column4: Full Form / Meaning
column6: Why Confusing for International Users
column7: Localization Recommendation

column4: British Columbia Occupational Health and Safety Regulation - the primary provincial workplace safety law in BC
column6: BC-specific provincial regulation; no equivalent by this name exists outside British Columbia. Non-Canadian users will not recognize the authority or citation format.
column7: Identify as British Columbia primary OH&S law; reference equivalent national/regional regulation in target country.

column4: Workers Compensation Board of British Columbia - provincial agency enforcing OHS regulations and administering workers compensation
column6: Unique to British Columbia; other provinces have different bodies (WSIB in Ontario / WCB in Alberta). Non-Canadian users will not recognize the authority.
column7: Explain as BC occupational safety enforcement and workers compensation body; identify equivalent authority for target country.

column4: Workplace Hazardous Materials Information System - Canada national hazard communication standard for workplace chemicals
column6: Canada-specific system (aligned with GHS since WHMIS 2015); called GHS or HazCom in other countries. The term itself is unrecognizable outside Canada.
column7: Define as Canada chemical hazard communication system; explain as equivalent to GHS/HazCom; note SDS and labeling requirements.

column4: Canadian Standards Association - Canada primary national standards development organization
column6: CSA standards (e.g. CSA Z94.4 for respirators / CSA Z107.56 for noise) are referenced throughout but are Canadian-specific. International users may use EN / ISO / ANSI / AS equivalents.
column7: Identify CSA as Canada national standards body; cross-reference ISO/EN equivalent standard where available.

column4: Ground Fault Circuit Interrupter - electrical safety device that cuts power when current leakage is detected
column6: North American term; called RCD (Residual Current Device) in UK/Europe/Australia or ELCB (Earth Leakage Circuit Breaker) in some Asian countries. Different trip thresholds apply.
column7: Define as Ground Fault Circuit Interrupter; note it is called RCD or ELCB in other regions; specify trip current threshold (typically 5 mA in North America).

column4: Immediately Dangerous to Life or Health - concentration of airborne contaminant posing immediate risk to life or causing irreversible health effects
column6: US NIOSH/OSHA term; equivalent concept exists internationally but under different names and threshold values. Not universally recognized outside North America.
column7: Define as the maximum concentration from which a worker could escape without injury; cross-reference national exposure limit standard in target country.

column4: Musculoskeletal Injury - injuries affecting muscles, tendons, ligaments, joints, nerves, or blood vessels from workplace physical demands
column6: BC-specific terminology for what other jurisdictions call MSD (Musculoskeletal Disorder) / RSI (Repetitive Strain Injury) / or WRMSDs (Work-Related Musculoskeletal Disorders).
column7: Define as workplace injury to muscles tendons or joints; note equivalent terms: MSD (US/EU) / RSI (UK/Australia) / WMSD in target language context.

column4: American Conference of Governmental Industrial Hygienists - US-based body publishing TLVs (Threshold Limit Values) for occupational exposures
column6: US organization whose TLV exposure limits are referenced in BC regulations but are not legal standards in most non-North American countries.
column7: Identify ACGIH as the US body setting TLVs; note that BC adopts ACGIH TLVs by reference; reference equivalent occupational exposure limits in target country.

column4: Threshold Limit Value - ACGIH occupational exposure guideline for airborne hazardous substances (expressed as TWA / STEL / or Ceiling)
column6: US ACGIH guideline adopted by BC regulation; other countries use OEL (EU) / MEL/WEL (UK) / MAK (Germany/Austria) / or national PELs. Values differ between systems.
column7: Define as ACGIH occupational exposure guideline; note BC adopts TLVs legally; cross-reference OEL or WEL or MAK for target country.

column4: Short-Term Exposure Limit - maximum airborne concentration allowed for a 15-minute period without causing acute harm
column6: Standard occupational hygiene term; 15-minute reference period is consistent globally but threshold values differ by substance and national standard.
column7: Define as 15-minute short-term exposure limit; specify that values are substance-specific and set by ACGIH TLVs in BC.

column4: Working Load Limit - the maximum mass a piece of rigging lifting or load-bearing equipment is rated to safely carry
column6: Term is used in BC but the concept is called SWL (Safe Working Load) in many countries (UK / Australia / EU). Calculation methods and certification requirements differ.
column7: Define as maximum rated load capacity; note it is equivalent to SWL (Safe Working Load) used in UK / EU / and Australia.

column4: Hydrogen Sulfide - toxic and flammable gas produced in oil/gas / mining / and wastewater operations
column6: Chemical formula is universally understood but regulatory exposure limits differ: BC uses ACGIH TLV of 1 ppm TWA; OSHA PEL is 20 ppm; EU OEL is 1 ppm.
column7: Define as Hydrogen Sulfide; state applicable exposure limit (ACGIH TLV 1 ppm TWA in BC); note life-threatening toxicity.

column4: Exposure Control Plan - documented program identifying hazards / exposure risks / and control measures for workplace biological or chemical hazards
column6: BC-specific regulatory term; equivalent documents are called COSHH (UK) / RSST plan (Quebec) / or Risk Management Plan in other jurisdictions.
column7: Define as documented plan identifying hazardous exposures and controls; identify equivalent document name in target country.

column4: Certified Faller - BC-specific licensed worker qualified to fell trees using chainsaws under BC forest industry safety certification system
column6: Term and certification are specific to BC/Canadian forest industry. Called chainsaw operator / tree feller / or lumberjack in other countries with different qualification systems.
column7: Define as a BC-certified tree-felling specialist; clarify that BC requires mandatory certification (BC Faller Training Standard); identify equivalent in target country.

column4: Qualified Arborist - specialist in tree care / pruning / and climbing certified under ISA (International Society of Arboriculture) or equivalent
column6: Term is used globally but certification bodies differ (ISA globally / Arboriculture Australia / Lantra in UK). BC references to qualified arborist require specific competency proof.
column7: Define as a certified tree care specialist; clarify required certification (ISA Certified Arborist or equivalent approved by WorkSafeBC).

column4: Rope access system - method of working at height using ropes and specialized equipment instead of scaffolding or aerial platforms
column6: Certification bodies differ: IRATA (International Rope Access Trade Association) in EU/internationally; SPRAT (Society of Professional Rope Access Technicians) in North America.
column7: Define as a height access system using ropes; specify required written plan contents; identify IRATA/SPRAT certification context.

column4: Raise Climber - specialized underground mining machine used to drill vertical or inclined shafts (raises) in hard rock mining
column6: Term is specific to Canadian hard rock mining; not used in most other countries or mining traditions. Equipment type and safety inspection requirements are BC-specific.
column7: Define as a machine for excavating vertical mine shafts; note BC-specific daily/weekly inspection requirement under BC OHS Regulation Part 22.

column4: Joint Occupational Health and Safety Committee - mandatory worker/employer safety committee required by BC law for workplaces with 20 or more workers
column6: BC name for what other countries call JHSC / safety committee / or works council. Composition and meeting frequency requirements differ.
column7: Define as mandatory joint worker-employer safety committee; clarify BC threshold (20+ workers) and monthly meeting requirement; identify equivalent body in target country.

column4: Legal right of BC workers under the Workers Compensation Act to refuse work they reasonably believe poses undue hazard to health or safety
column6: Right exists in many jurisdictions but procedures for exercising refusal / investigation steps / and protection from reprisal vary significantly by country.
column7: Define as a BC statutory right; outline the investigation procedure steps; note equivalent right in target country labour law.

column4: Worker holding a valid blasting certificate issued by WorkSafeBC / legally required to conduct or direct all blasting operations in BC
column6: BC-specific certification system; other countries have different licensing bodies (US: ATF/state / UK: ShotBlaster certification / Australia: state-based licensing).
column7: Define as a WorkSafeBC-licensed explosives specialist; clarify BC requirement that all blasting must be supervised by a certified blaster; identify equivalent license in target country.

column4: Mandatory written record of pre-blast conditions and post-blast site status required under BC OHS Regulation; retained minimum 5 years
column6: BC-specific documentation requirement; record format / retention period / and required content differ from other jurisdictions.
column7: Define as mandatory blasting record; specify 5-year retention and required content per BC OHS Regulation Part 21.

column4: Lockout/Tagout - energy isolation procedure using physical locks and tags to prevent accidental re-energization of equipment during maintenance
column6: Concept is global but called LOTO (Lock-Out/Tag-Out) in the US / Isolation in Australia/NZ / or Lock-Off in the UK. BC specifies group lockout and personal lock requirements.
column7: Define as energy isolation using physical locks; note BC requires personal lock control and group lockout for multi-worker tasks; identify equivalent procedure in target country.

column4: Minimum safe working distances from energized high-voltage electrical conductors / set by BC OHS Regulation to prevent arc flash or contact
column6: BC-specific regulatory distances (BC OHS Regulation Part 19 Table 19-1); approach distance thresholds differ by voltage level and by national electrical standards (NFPA 70E / IEC / etc.).
column7: Define as minimum required distance from live electrical lines; state that distances are voltage-dependent per BC OHS Regulation Table 19-1; identify equivalent standard in target country.

column4: Excavation protective systems: sloping = angled cut; shoring = structural support; benching = stepped cuts - BC specifies methods by soil type
column6: Required method depends on soil classification and depth; BC uses soil Type 1-4 which differs from US OSHA Type A/B/C classification.
column7: Define each method; clarify BC soil type classification system; note difference from OSHA Type A/B/C classification used in US.

column4: BOP - mechanical device installed on oil/gas wells to seal the wellbore and prevent uncontrolled release of formation fluids
column6: Highly specialized oil/gas drilling term; design standards (API 16A/53) / pressure ratings / and testing requirements set by international petroleum industry standards.
column7: Define as emergency pressure control equipment for oil/gas wells; reference API standard 16A/53; note this applies only to oil and gas drilling operations.

column4: Controlled ascent protocol used in diving and hyperbaric work to prevent decompression sickness by allowing dissolved gases to exit safely
column6: Dive tables and decompression algorithms vary (US Navy tables / DCIEM / PADI/recreational limits); BC uses specific pressurized work regulations.
column7: Define as mandatory ascent/pressure reduction protocol; reference BC OHS Regulation Part 24 and DCIEM tables used in Canada.

column4: Decibels C-weighted - sound measurement scale used for peak noise levels (e.g. impact noise); complements dBA for hearing conservation
column6: Less commonly known than dBA; BC specifies 140 dBC peak limit; other countries may use different peak measurement scales or thresholds.
column7: Define as C-weighted decibel scale for peak impulse noise; state BC limit (140 dBC peak); explain difference from dBA used for continuous noise.

column4: Supplied-Air Respirator - respiratory protection device that delivers clean breathing air from a remote source via hose
column6: Distinct from SCBA; performance standards differ between CSA (Canada) / NIOSH (US) / and EN 14594 (EU); required for IDLH atmospheres in BC.
column7: Define as airline respirator supplying breathing air from a remote compressor; distinguish from SCBA; reference CSA Z94.4 and note NIOSH/EN equivalents.

column4: ANSI S2.70 / ISO 2631-1 - standards for measuring and evaluating human whole-body vibration exposure
column6: BC references both US ANSI and international ISO standards; EU Directive 2002/44/EC uses different action/limit values.
column7: Identify ANSI S2.70 as US standard and ISO 2631-1 as international; note BC uses both for vibration assessment; reference EU Directive 2002/44/EC limits for European target countries.

column4: BC regulatory requirement to provide heated rest shelters when outdoor temperatures fall below -7 degrees Celsius for cold weather work
column6: Temperature threshold is BC/Canadian specific; other countries use different trigger temperatures or have no specific heated shelter requirement.
column7: State threshold as -7 degrees C (19 degrees F); explain this is a BC-specific cold weather protection requirement; identify equivalent cold stress provisions in target country.

column4: Obligation under BC OHS Regulation Part 6 for employers to offer hepatitis B vaccination at no cost to workers at risk of biological exposure
column6: Vaccination offer obligation is BC-specific; other countries have different obligations under different regulatory frameworks (OSHA Bloodborne Pathogen Standard in US / EU Directive 2000/54/EC).
column7: Define as employer-funded vaccination obligation; reference equivalent bloodborne pathogen protection requirement in target country.

column4: Minimum safe separation distances from RF transmitters when using electric or electronic detonators during blasting operations
column6: Distances are defined in BC OHS Regulation Part 21 based on transmitter power; different standards apply in other countries (EU EN 13763 / US ISEE); RF frequency bands also differ.
column7: Define as safety buffer distances from radio transmitters during blasting; reference BC OHS Regulation Part 21 table; identify equivalent standard for target country.

column4: Documented written plan required for crane lifts meeting specific risk criteria (near capacity / multiple cranes / over workers)
column6: Term is used in North America; called engineered lift plan or complex lift plan in other countries; trigger criteria and required plan contents differ by jurisdiction and crane standard.
column7: Define as a mandatory written plan for high-risk crane operations; specify BC trigger criteria; identify equivalent planning requirement in target country.

column4: Rigging: hardware and technique for attaching loads to cranes/hoists. Rigger: qualified worker certified to attach loads for lifting operations
column6: Rigging qualification requirements differ: BC requires qualified rigger familiar with standard hand signals; other countries have different certification bodies (LEEA in UK / ASME B30.9 in US).
column7: Define rigging as the hardware and method for attaching loads; define rigger as the qualified specialist; reference LEEA/ASME equivalent in target country.

column4: Signed written authorization document completed before each entry into a high-hazard confined space under BC OHS Regulation
column6: Terminology varies: work permit / confined space entry permit / authorization to enter; required signatories and content differ by jurisdiction.
column7: Define as the required written authorization before confined space entry; specify BC requirements for content (hazards / controls / rescue); identify equivalent permit in target country.

column4: Single worker-elected safety representative required by BC law for workplaces with 10 to 19 workers (below joint committee threshold)
column6: BC-specific role; other jurisdictions use different titles and thresholds: safety officer / health and safety rep (UK) / delegue du personnel (France); minimum workforce size trigger differs.
column7: Define as a worker-elected safety liaison for smaller BC workplaces (10-19 workers); identify equivalent worker representation role in target country.

column4: Mandatory audible warning sounds (siren/horn pattern) required before and after blasting to alert workers to evacuate and return
column6: Signal patterns (number of blasts / duration) are specified in BC OHS Regulation Part 21; patterns may differ from other jurisdictions or industry practices.
column7: Define as mandatory warning sounds before and after detonation; specify BC signal pattern requirements; note that patterns may differ from target country standards.

column4: BC regulatory trigger height of 3 meters at which fall protection becomes mandatory for most general industry work
column6: BC-specific 3-meter (approx 10 ft) threshold; US OSHA uses 4 ft (1.2 m) for general industry / 6 ft (1.8 m) for construction; EU and other countries vary.
column7: State BC threshold as 3 meters; note this differs from US OSHA (1.2-1.8 m) and EU standards; clarify equivalent trigger height in target country.

column4: Worker designated to give standardized hand or communication signals to crane/equipment operators during lifting or aircraft operations
column6: BC requires use of internationally recognized signals and high-visibility apparel; terminology (signaller vs signalman vs banksman) and signal standards differ by country.
column7: Define as the designated worker giving lift/movement signals; clarify BC requirement for internationally recognized signals and high-visibility clothing; note banksman is the UK equivalent term.

column1: # NOTE: The following terms were excluded because they already exist in AvettaGlossary.xlsx (col M de_de / col AA en_us):
# OHS | SDS | HEPA | TWA | Fall arrest system | Hot work permit | Vibration exposure limits
